flashcards.back: A discrete probability distribution of the number of successes in a sequence of n independent experiments, each asking a yes–no question, and each with its own boolean-valued outcome: success/yes/true/one (with probability p) or failure/no/false/zero (with probability 1 − p).

flashcards.front: What is n in Binomial Distribution?
flashcards.back: The number of trials or experiments

flashcards.front: What is p in Binomial Distribution?
flashcards.back: The probability of success in a single trial

flashcards.front: What is the mean of a Binomial Distribution?
flashcards.back: np, where n is the number of trials and p is the probability of success
